summary:
Iter 79j.4 snap-close verified end-to-end on both Mask (step 4) and Profile (step 5) in guided-capture mode. Logged in as Howard, opened Contractor Quotes → Vinyl+Ascend, created a fresh estimate, launched AI Measure → Guided Capture, uploaded /tmp/dummy.png via data-testid='guided-capture-input-0', and walked Next → Skip → Skip to Mask. Verified: (a) Mask banner text INCLUDES the phrase 'tap your first polygon point again to close' — confirmed via body innerText scan. (b) Selected Polygon toggle, tapped 4 corner points on the drawing SVG. The DOM showed 5 <circle> elements (4 vertex dots + 1 non-fill ring around the first vertex with r=12 vs r=7 for the first vertex fill circle — i.e., the enlarged first vertex ring for canSnap-ready state, exactly as coded). (c) Computed the true screen coord of the first vertex from viewBox '0 0 600 400' and svg client-rect (654x436), then clicked EXACTLY on it — the polygon-preview dashed path (stroke-dasharray='8 4') dropped from 1 to 0 AND the committed 'NO SIDING' polygon appeared with solid orange stroke + hatch fill in the photo canvas (screenshot /tmp/mask_snapped2.png). polyPoints cleared, snap-close committed the zone. (d) Rectangle 2-tap regression: switched back to Rectangle, clicked two corners — no console errors, mode functional. (e) Advanced Skip → Profile step 5. Banner text INCLUDES 'Tap your first polygon point again to close' — confirmed. Repeated polygon placement (4 pts) + click-exact-first-vertex → dashed preview cleared, committed 'SHAKE · 50 ft²' profile box appeared in the canvas AND in the 'Profile boxes (1)' panel with body/50 defaults (screenshot /tmp/profile_snapped2.png). (f) Rectangle 2-tap on Profile also works.

EARLIER TEST NOTE (important for main agent): The first snap-close attempt using page.mouse.click(p1_screen + 3px, p1_screen + 3px) FAILED — a 5th point was added instead of snap-closing. Root cause: the photo's viewBox is 600x400 but the rendered SVG is 654x436, so photo-px = screen-px × (600/654) ≈ 0.917. A +3 screen-px offset ≈ 2.75 photo-px, well under the 18-photo-px threshold — BUT there's an additional offset from the SVG's bounding-box that my bbox computation ignored (the modal padding shifted the actual svg.y by ~16 px from the container bbox). Clicking at the EXACT first-vertex screen coord (derived from viewBox + getBoundingClientRect) snap-closed reliably on both Mask and Profile. In real contractor usage on iPad/desktop where the user visually taps ON the enlarged first vertex circle, this will always land within the 18-photo-px threshold, so behavior is correct. No action item for main agent — just noting that automated Playwright coord math needs to derive from the SVG viewBox, not the container bbox.

VERIFIED features from review-request checklist: (1) snap-close on Mask ✓, (2) snap-close on Profile ✓, (3) visual cue = enlarged first vertex (r=12 vs r=7 initial) + ring rendered ✓, (4) banner copy on Mask ✓ and Profile ✓, (5) Rectangle regression on both steps ✓, (6) 5-step guided sequence + skip/next buttons unaffected ✓ (walked step1→2→3→4→5 without issue).
critical_code_review_comments: PhotoAnnotateModal.jsx line 636-642 _isNearFirstPoint uses zoom-aware threshold Math.max(8, 18/zoom) in photo-px — correct implementation, tested working. Consider adding a data-testid='annotate-first-vertex-ring' on the ring <circle> at ~line 972 so future test agents can query the snap-ready state deterministically instead of counting circles.

PhotoAnnotateModal.jsx snap-close paths (MODE_PROFILE ~line 580, MODE_ZONE ~line 620) directly commit and clear polyPoints — no undo/redo hook. If Howard mis-snaps, contractor must draw the polygon again. Not a bug but a UX enhancement to consider.
Carried from iter 29: File is now 1751 lines (was 1689) — well past the 700-line guideline. Extract <MaskModeControls />, <ProfileModeControls />, and <PolygonPreview /> subcomponents.

Carried from iter 28: handleGuidedNext still advances setGuidedStepIdx unconditionally on step 1 (Wall Measurement — 'required'). User can Next past it without setting the wall scale ref. Note: on my run I did click Next through step 1 without setting scale and the Profile sqft defaulted to 50 (sentinel from _computeProfileSqft when no ref set) — banner warns 'Set Wall Anchor first', so behavior is acceptable but not enforced.

Minor test-time note: the polygon 'Close (N pts)' fallback button was not conclusively verified because get_by_role('button', name='Close', exact=False) is ambiguous with the modal's aria-labelled close X. If main agent wants to strengthen tests, add data-testid='annotate-polygon-close-btn' to the closePolygon button so this can be regressed cleanly.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — snap-close on Mask ✓, snap-close on Profile ✓, banner text on both ✓, Rectangle regression on both ✓, guided nav ✓, visual cue (enlarged first vertex + ring) rendered ✓

seed_data_creation: Fresh empty Vinyl+Ascend estimate created via 'New Estimate' on /dashboard/siding. Uploaded /tmp/dummy.png (600x400 PNG). One committed masked zone (Brick 'NO SIDING' polygon) and one committed profile box (SHAKE polygon, 50 ft² sentinel) were created during snap-close verification and remain saved on the fresh estimate.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SNAP-CLOSE WORKS. To reliably click ON the first polygon vertex, do NOT use the SVG container bounding_box() — the container may include padding/margin that shifts the SVG origin. Instead, read the actual <circle> cx/cy from 'svg.absolute.inset-0' and translate via svg.viewBox + svg.getBoundingClientRect(): screen_x = rect.x + cx * (rect.width / vbWidth). This gave pixel-perfect vertex targeting on both Mask and Profile. The overlay SVG has class 'absolute inset-0 w-full h-full pointer-events-none' — clicks are captured by the parent photo-container div and mapped to photo coords by the modal's click handler. Snap-close threshold in code: Math.max(8, 18/zoom) photo-px. At default 100% zoom, that's 18 photo-px which maps to ~19.6 screen-px on the tested layout (600->654 scale). To exercise the classic Close (N pts) button as a fallback regression: main agent should add data-testid='annotate-polygon-close-btn' to that button in PhotoAnnotateModal.jsx closePolygon path, since 'Close' role-name matcher collides with the modal's own X-close button.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature is working. Root cause of Iter 30's initial FAIL (5 pts placed instead of snap) was a test-harness bug: I derived click coords from svg.bounding_box() which included a 16-px vertical padding offset the actual <svg> element does not have. Once I switched to reading vertex cx/cy from the SVG's own viewBox/rect, snap-close fired 100% reliably on both Mask and Profile.
